summary.en: Thomas J. Chermack's organizational guide to creating, using, and assessing scenarios through a performance-based scenario system, from project preparation to assessment.
summary.zh: Thomas J. Chermack 关于组织如何创建、使用和评估情景的指南，以基于绩效的情景系统贯穿项目准备、探索、开发、实施和评估。

recommendation.en: Read this after learning basic scenario thinking. It explains how to run scenario planning as an organizational project, not just how to draw a scenario matrix.
recommendation.zh: 在理解基础情景思维后阅读这本。它解释如何把情景规划作为组织项目来运作，而不只是画一张情景矩阵。

sources.label: Thomas J. Chermack · Scenario Planning in Organizations: How to Create, Use, and Assess Scenarios · Berrett-Koehler Publishers · 2011 · ISBN 978-1605094137
Amazon · Scenario Planning in Organizations product page

headline.en: Make scenario planning usable inside organizations
headline.zh: 让情景规划在组织里真正可用

whyOpen.en: Use this when scenario work must influence meetings, strategy routines, facilitation, and performance conversations.
whyOpen.zh: 当情景工作需要影响会议、战略流程、引导和绩效讨论时读这本。

audience.en: Strategy facilitators, leadership teams, planners, and teams embedding uncertainty work into routines.
audience.zh: 适合战略引导师、领导团队、规划人员，以及要把不确定性工作嵌入流程的团队。

commonMisreads.en: Scenarios are not forecasts; they are structured alternatives for learning and preparing decisions.
commonMisreads.zh: 情景不是预测；它们是帮助学习和准备决策的结构化替代未来。

firstSteps.en: Start with the scenario matrix only after clarifying the decision that needs uncertainty thinking.
firstSteps.zh: 先明确需要不确定性思考的决策，再使用情景矩阵。

firstSteps.en: Plan how scenarios will change recurring meetings, not only workshop output.
firstSteps.zh: 规划情景如何改变周期性会议，而不只是产出工作坊文件。

outcomes.en: You can design a scenario process that changes decisions, signals, and organizational routines.
outcomes.zh: 你能设计一个会改变决策、信号和组织流程的情景规划过程。

practicePrompts.en: Which strategic decision in your organization would improve if the team held two futures in mind?
practicePrompts.zh: 你的组织里，哪一个战略决策会因为团队同时思考两个未来而变好？
